stories.title: Create BRAND-has-video Relationship
stories.userStory:
As an API contributor
I want to be able to attach VIDEOS to a BRAND
So an API consumer can illustrate the BRAND node in a frontend application
specificationList: The relationship ›has-video‹ is created when the provided IDs of the BRAND and VIDEO are valid. -> Status Code `201`
A successful request returns the ID of the created relationship, the relationship name and the relationship partner.
Requests with invalid IDs are rejected. This can happen when there exist no nodes with the given IDs or when those nodes are from the wrong type. -> Status Code `404`
The same ›has-video‹ relationship between the same nodes can only be created once. Attempts to recreate them will be ignored. -> Status Code `304`
Each BRAND can be in a ›has-video‹ relationship with multiple VIDEOS

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to create a ›has-video‹ relationship are accepted when the provided data is valid
acceptanceCriteria.description: To be valid there need to exist nodes for the provided IDs and the nodes need to be from type BRAND resp. VIDEO.

tests.title: Creating a ›has-video‹ relationship with valid IDs
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 201
And the response should contain the following keys
| key                  |
| relationship_id      |
| relationship_name    |
| start_node           |
| partner_node         |

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to create a ›has-video‹ relationship are rejected when the provided data is invalid
acceptanceCriteria.description:
There exists no BRAND with the provided ID? -> rejected.
There exists no VIDEO with the provided ID? -> rejected.

tests.title: Trying to create a ›has-video‹ relationship with invalid BRAND ID
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

tests.title: Trying to create a ›has-video‹ relationship with invalid VIDEO ID
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And "VIDEO" "Promo Video" does NOT exist
When the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

tests.title: Trying to create a ›has-video‹ relationship where both IDs are invalid
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
And "VIDEO" "Promo Video" does NOT exist
When the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

acceptanceCriteria.title: The same ›has-video‹ relationship between the same nodes can only be created once
acceptanceCriteria.description: Requests to create the same relationship again are allowed, but they will not be processed. Consecutive attempts will always return an empty 304 response.

tests.title: Trying to create the same ›has-video‹ relationship again
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
And there exists a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the response should return with status code 304

acceptanceCriteria.title: Each BRAND can be in a ›has-video‹ relationship with multiple VIDEOS
acceptanceCriteria.description: There is no limit on the amount of relationships, they just need to be unique.

tests.title: Creating multiple ›has-video‹ relationships
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video (Part 2)"
When the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
And the user creates a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video (Part 2)"
Then there should exist a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
And there should exist a "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video (Part 2)"

stories.title: Get all BRAND-has-video Relationships
stories.userStory:
As an API consumer
I want to be able to fetch all VIDEOS that are connected to a BRAND
So I can find out more about it - be it in form of a review, a promo video or a podcast
specificationList: A list of all ›has-video‹ relationships for the selected BRAND is returned when there exists at least one of them. -> Status Code `200`
Each item in the list contains the ID of the created relationship, the relationship name and the relationship partner.
An empty list is returned when no ›has-video‹ relationship exists. -> Status Code `200`
Requests with invalid ID are rejected. This can happen when there exists no node with the given ID or it is not a BRAND. -> Status Code `404`

acceptanceCriteria.title: A list of all ›has-video‹ relationships is returned when the provided data is valid
acceptanceCriteria.description: To be a valid request the provided ID has to point to a BRAND node that actually exists.

tests.title: Requesting the ›has-video‹ relationships when at least one exists
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exist 3 "has-video" relationships for "BMW"
When the user requests all "has-video" relationships for "BMW"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 200
And the response should return a collection with 3 "has-video" relationships

acceptanceCriteria.title: An empty list is returned when there exist no ›has-video‹ relationships for the given BRAND

tests.title: Requesting the ›has-video‹ relationships when there are none
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exist 0 "has-video" relationships for "BMW"
When the user requests all "has-video" relationships for "BMW"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 200
And the response should return an empty list

acceptanceCriteria.title: A request to fetch all ›has-video‹ relationships is rejected when the provided data is invalid
acceptanceCriteria.description: There exists no BRAND with the provided ID? -> rejected.

tests.title: Trying to fetch the ›has-video‹ relationships with an invalid BRAND ID
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
When the user requests all "has-video" relationships for "BMW"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

stories.title: Delete BRAND-has-video Relationship
stories.userStory:
As an API contributor
I want to be able to disconnect VIDEOS from BRANDS
So I can clean up bad data or test data
specificationList: The ›has-video‹ relationship is deleted when it actually exists. -> Status Code `204`
A successful request returns with an empty response.
Requests with invalid IDs are rejected. This can happen when there exist no nodes with the given IDs or when those nodes are from the wrong type. -> Status Code `404`
Requests are rejected when there exists no ›has-video‹ relationship between the provided nodes. -> Status Code `404`

acceptanceCriteria.title: The ›has-video‹ relationship is deleted when the provided data is valid
acceptanceCriteria.description: To be valid there need to exist nodes for the provided IDs and the first node needs to be a BRAND and the second node needs to be a VIDEO.

tests.title: Deleting the ›has-video‹ relationship when it actually exists
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
And there exists a "has-video" relationship "R" between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
When the user deletes the "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 204
And the relationship "R" should not exist anymore

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to delete the ›has-video‹ relationship are rejected when the provided data is invalid
acceptanceCriteria.description:
There exists no BRAND with the provided ID? -> rejected.
There exists no VIDEO with the provided ID? -> rejected.

tests.title: Trying to delete a ›has-video‹ relationship with invalid BRAND ID
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
When the user deletes the "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

tests.title: Trying to delete a ›has-video‹ relationship with invalid VIDEO ID
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And "VIDEO" "Promo Video" does NOT exist
When the user deletes the "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

tests.title: Trying to delete a ›has-video‹ relationship where both IDs are invalid
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
And "VIDEO" "Promo Video" does NOT exist
When the user deletes the "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to delete the ›has-video‹ relationship are rejected when the relationship does not exist
acceptanceCriteria.description: The relationship might have never existed or it has already been deleted. No matter the reason, an error will be returned.

tests.title: Trying to delete a non-existent ›has-video‹ relationship
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
And there exists NO "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
When the user deletes the "has-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

stories.title: Create BRAND-has-main-video Relationship
stories.userStory:
As an API contributor
I want to be able to select the main VIDEO of a BRAND
So an API consumer can instantly load the VIDEO that best represents the BRAND
specificationList: The relationship ›has-main-video‹ is created when the provided IDs of the BRAND and VIDEO are valid. -> Status Code `201`
A successful request returns the ID of the created relationship, the relationship name and the relationship partner.
Requests with invalid IDs are rejected. This can happen when there exist no nodes with the given IDs or when those nodes are from the wrong type. -> Status Code `404`
The same ›has-main-video‹ relationship between the same nodes can only be created once. Attempts to recreate them will be ignored. -> Status Code `304`
At the same time a BRAND can only be in a ›has-main-video‹ relationship with one VIDEO

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to create a ›has-main-video‹ relationship are accepted when the provided data is valid
acceptanceCriteria.description: To be valid there need to exist nodes for the provided IDs and the nodes need to be from type BRAND resp. VIDEO.

tests.title: Creating a ›has-main-video‹ relationship with valid IDs
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 201
And the response should contain the following keys
| key                  |
| relationship_id      |
| relationship_name    |
| start_node           |
| partner_node         |

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to create a ›has-main-video‹ relationship are rejected when the provided data is invalid
acceptanceCriteria.description:
There exists no BRAND with the provided ID? -> rejected.
There exists no VIDEO with the provided ID? -> rejected.

tests.title: Trying to create a ›has-main-video‹ relationship with invalid BRAND ID
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

tests.title: Trying to create a ›has-main-video‹ relationship with invalid VIDEO ID
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And "VIDEO" "Promo Video" does NOT exist
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

tests.title: Trying to create a ›has-main-video‹ relationship where both IDs are invalid
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
And "VIDEO" "Promo Video" does NOT exist
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404

acceptanceCriteria.title: The same ›has-main-video‹ relationship between the same nodes can only be created once
acceptanceCriteria.description: Requests to create the same relationship again are allowed, but they will not be processed. Consecutive attempts will always return an empty 304 response.

tests.title: Trying to create the same ›has-main-video‹ relationship again
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
And there exists a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then the response should return with status code 304

acceptanceCriteria.title: At the same time, each BRAND can only be in a ›has-main-video‹ relationship with one VIDEO
acceptanceCriteria.description: Connecting a different VIDEO will delete the previous relationship.

tests.title: Creating a ›has-main-video‹ relationship with a different VIDEO
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video"
And there exists a "VIDEO" "Promo Video (Part 2)"
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
Then there should exist a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"
When the user creates a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video (Part 2)"
Then there should exist a "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video (Part 2)"
But there should exist NO "has-main-video" relationship between "BMW" and "Promo Video"

stories.title: Get BRAND-has-main-video Relationship
stories.userStory:
As an API consumer
I want to be able to get the main VIDEO of a BRAND
So I can instantly load the VIDEO that best represents the BRAND
specificationList: The ›has-main-video‹ relationship for the selected BRAND is returned when one exists. -> Status Code `200`
The response contains the relationship ID, the relationship name and the relationship partner.
An empty response is returned when the selected BRAND has no ›has-main-video‹ relationship. -> Status Code `200`
Requests with invalid ID are rejected. This can happen when there exists no node with the given ID or it is not a BRAND. -> Status Code `404`

acceptanceCriteria.title: The ›has-main-video‹ relationship is returned when the provided data is valid
acceptanceCriteria.description: To be a valid request the provided ID has to point to a BRAND node that actually exists.

tests.title: Requesting the ›has-main-video‹ relationship when one exists
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists a "has-main-video" relationship "R" for "BMW"
When the user requests the "has-main-video" relationship for "BMW"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 200
And the response should return the relationship "R"

acceptanceCriteria.title: An empty response is returned when there exists no ›has-main-video‹ relationship for the given BRAND

tests.title: Requesting the ›has-main-video‹ relationship when there exists none
tests.gherkin:
Given there exists a "BRAND" "BMW"
And there exists NO "has-main-video" relationship for "BMW"
When the user requests the "has-main-video" relationship for "BMW"
Then the request should be confirmed with status code 200
But the response should return an empty body

acceptanceCriteria.title: Requests to fetch the ›has-main-video‹ relationship are rejected when the provided data is invalid
acceptanceCriteria.description: There exists no BRAND with the provided ID? -> rejected.

tests.title: Trying to fetch the ›has-main-video‹ relationship with an invalid BRAND ID
tests.gherkin:
Given "BRAND" "BMW" does NOT exist
When the user requests the "has-main-video" relationship for "BMW"
Then the request should be rejected with status code 404
